issues.finding: REQ-002-AC-4 says 'merge incrementally, not overwrite' but does not define merge conflict resolution strategy when incremental merge produces contradictions (e.g., domain model entity renamed vs new entity with old name). Only entity name collision is handled in REQ-008, not general merge conflicts across L0/L1/L3.

REQ-003-AC-1 says 'read relevant profile layers' but does not specify which layers are relevant for which stage. Discover, spec, and build have different context needs. A developer could inject all layers everywhere or only L0 — both interpretations are valid.

REQ-004-AC-3 first-time onboarding generates L0/L1/L3 but does not specify what happens to L2. If L2 flag is enabled, should first-time onboarding attempt to extract intent from existing code? The requirement is silent. This is an edge case that could cause confusion.
REQ-005 has 7 acceptance criteria covering three different stages (discover, spec, build). This is a compound requirement — it would be cleaner split per stage, but does not cause correctness issues.

REQ-006-AC-2 references a 'configured threshold' for large codebase splitting but does not define: (a) what the threshold metric is (file count, LOC, directory depth), (b) where the threshold is configured, (c) what the default value is. This makes testability difficult.
issues.escalation_reason: Vagueness in concreteness directly makes completeness indeterminate — cannot verify correct threshold behavior without knowing what is being thresholded

issues.finding: REQ-006-AC-1 lists 'tech stack, directory structure, module dependencies, test coverage summary' but does not define what constitutes a 'module dependency' in a language-agnostic way. For TypeScript this likely means import graphs, but the requirement doesn't specify.

REQ-007 only handles conflict with L2 (design philosophy, constraints, architecture decisions). No mechanism exists for detecting conflicts with L0/L1 structural constraints (e.g., feature requires a runtime the project doesn't use, or violates existing module boundary). REQ-014-AC-2 provides graceful degradation when L2 is absent, but no requirement specifies conflict detection against L0/L1.

REQ-008-AC-3 says domain model merges into 'profile L3 domain model snapshot', but the domain_model entity in index.json is defined at the discover level (index.json line 95-119), not as part of L3-status. The domain model in the discover schema is a top-level field. If L3 is supposed to contain a domain model snapshot, this needs to be explicit in the L3 layer definition (StatusLayer entity only mentions 'test coverage, tech debt, change hotspots, domain model snapshot' — the snapshot part is mentioned but the relationship to the discover-level domain_model is unclear).
REQ-011-AC-1 says 'dependency direction changes' but does not define what constitutes a direction violation in the context of NoPilot's architecture. Minor — spec phase can define this.

REQ-015-AC-1 uses 'significantly earlier' without defining a threshold. What constitutes 'significant'? 1 hour? 1 day? 10 commits? This is untestable as written — no test can determine when to trigger the warning without a concrete threshold or heuristic definition.
issues.escalation_reason: Concreteness failure directly makes the acceptance criterion untestable, which blocks completeness — cannot verify correct behavior without knowing the trigger threshold

issues.finding: REQ-015-AC-1 references 'profile.updated_at' but no existing schema defines this field, and REQ-001 (profile storage) does not mention a timestamp field in the profile structure. The updated_at field needs to be added to REQ-001 or defined here.

findings.description: Missing invariant: Profile updated_at timestamp SHALL be written on every profile write operation. REQ-015 depends on this field existing but no invariant or requirement guarantees it is maintained.

Missing invariant: Profile merge operations SHALL be idempotent — running the same build twice SHALL NOT produce duplicate entries. REQ-002-AC-4 specifies incremental merge but no invariant guards against duplication.
INV-005 is scoped 'module-specific' which is correct — it only applies to the scanning module (REQ-006). All other invariants are correctly scoped as 'system-wide'.

No contradictions found between invariants. INV-001 (greenfield unchanged) and INV-002 (L2 independent) are complementary. INV-003 (reference not copy) and INV-004 (no spec modification) are orthogonal.

findings.reason: Cannot derive a concrete test without knowing: (a) the threshold metric, (b) the threshold value, (c) where the threshold is configured. 'Configured threshold' is a placeholder, not a specification.

'Significantly earlier' is not testable. No concrete time delta, commit count, or heuristic is defined. A test would need to pick an arbitrary threshold, which defeats the purpose of acceptance criteria.
'Relevant profile layers' is ambiguous — which layers are relevant for which stage? A test would need to enumerate the mapping, but the requirement does not provide it.

'Profile read operation SHALL complete within 2 seconds' — testable in principle but the measurement boundary is unclear (does this include disk I/O only, or also JSON parsing and injection into the prompt context?).
'Record the acknowledged staleness as a known risk' — where is it recorded? In the feature's discover.json? In the profile? In a log? Testable only if the storage location is specified.

findings.description: All three scenarios (SCENARIO-001, SCENARIO-002, SCENARIO-003) are covered by requirements. SCENARIO-001 maps to REQ-001,002,004,005,013. SCENARIO-002 maps to REQ-003,004,005,006,007,008,009,010,011,012,014,015. SCENARIO-003 maps to REQ-004,006.
No orphan requirements. All 15 requirements are referenced by at least one scenario.

SCENARIO-003 (first-time onboarding) references only REQ-004 and REQ-006 but the scenario description implies profile creation (REQ-001) is also exercised. This is not a block — REQ-001 is infrastructure used implicitly — but the mapping could be more explicit.

SCENARIO-002 does not reference REQ-001 (storage) or REQ-002 (writer), yet the scenario description ends with 'profile update' which exercises both. Similarly, REQ-013 (L2 intent extraction) is not referenced but could apply during SCENARIO-002's build phase. Again not a block — these are infrastructure requirements exercised implicitly.
REQ-015 (staleness warning) has no corresponding entry in mvp_features (F1-F14 map to REQ-001 through REQ-014 only). REQ-015 is an orphan in the MVP feature list.

patterns_found.evidence: REQ-006 downstream_impact says 'effort_estimate: 2-3 days' for Agent-driven codebase scanning with sub-agent parallelization. Scanning arbitrary codebases with correct module dependency extraction is substantially harder than 2-3 days, especially for polyglot projects.

REQ-007 downstream_impact says 'effort_estimate: 2-3 days' for LLM-based semantic conflict detection. The pre-mortem in index.json itself warns about false positives eroding trust, yet the effort estimate doesn't account for tuning precision.

No requirement addresses what happens when profile write fails mid-operation (e.g., disk full, permission error, corrupted JSON). Profile could be left in a partially-written state with no recovery mechanism specified.

REQ-004 intent detection has no fallback for ambiguous cases — what if the directory has code AND git history but the code is a template/skeleton with no real logic? The two-dimensional detection (profile x code) doesn't handle this edge case.

self_fix_log.description: Attempted to fix two block-severity findings: REQ-006-AC-2 vague threshold and REQ-015-AC-1 vague staleness threshold, plus REQ-015 missing updated_at dependency on REQ-001.
fixes_applied: REQ-006-AC-2: Recommend changing to 'WHILE codebase file count exceeds a configurable threshold (default: 500 files), THE SYSTEM SHALL split scanning across sub-agents per top-level directory and merge results. The threshold SHALL be configurable via .nopilot/config.json.'

REQ-015-AC-1: Recommend changing to 'WHEN profile is read and profile.updated_at is more than 24 hours older than the latest git commit timestamp, THE SYSTEM SHALL warn the user and recommend regenerating the profile. The threshold SHALL be configurable via .nopilot/config.json.'
REQ-001: Recommend adding AC-6: 'EACH profile layer file SHALL include an updated_at ISO 8601 timestamp, written on every profile write operation.'
self_fix_log.note: Critic is operating in independent review mode per task instructions. Fixes are recorded as recommendations. The block findings require author action.

trend_evaluation.detail: Single iteration — recommendations provided for all block findings. No trend to evaluate.
